{"key_event":"Authoritarian socialism and Ogaden defeat","reasoning":"Barre’s one-party socialist regime, nationalizations, security courts, literacy campaigns, and Soviet-backed militarization deepened state penetration, though the Ogaden defeat, loss of Soviet support, fiscal strain, and armed exile opposition left capacity and cohesion below its mid-decade peak. Independent civil society fell sharply as parties, press, unions, elders, religious critics, and private economic actors were repressed or absorbed into state channels, with post-1978 resistance organized mainly through fragmented clan-military networks rather than open civic institutions.","society_change":-1.2,"society_power":3.8,"state_change":0.5,"state_power":6.9}
